summary: Verified image loading performance optimizations across Living Heritage archive. Hero image uses eager loading + fetchpriority=high, all other images across HomePage, CollectionsPage, and CollectionDetailPage (Rajwada) correctly use loading='lazy'. Page loads quickly with FCP ~2.1s and DOMContentLoaded ~1.6s. Progressive lazy loading attributes verified on all non-critical img tags.
passed: Homepage hero has loading='eager' and fetchpriority='high'
Homepage has 4 non-hero images with loading='lazy' (1 eager, 4 lazy)
Hero image fully rendered by ~2.10s from navigation start
CollectionsPage: all 3 collection card images have loading='lazy'
CollectionDetailPage /collections/rajwada: all 9 archive grid images have loading='lazy'
Page interactive quickly; no blocking JavaScript observed
Scroll to bottom of Rajwada page works smoothly, all images available
observations: On Rajwada detail page, browser eagerly resolved all 9 lazy images even before scroll — this is expected Chromium behavior (lazy-loading heuristics preload nearby images especially when total page length is modest). The loading='lazy' attribute is correctly applied; browser retains discretion on actual deferral.
Only 5 img tags found on homepage — Explore/Voices sections may use CSS backgrounds or fewer images than expected, but this matches the source (1 hero + 4 lazy).
